_doc: Labeled query set for calibrating RERANK_SCORE_FLOOR (kb.ts). NOT used at runtime.
label = what the corpus SHOULD do for this query:
'grounded'   -> the corpus covers this; the top rerank score SHOULD clear the floor.
'off_corpus' -> the corpus does NOT cover this; the top score SHOULD fall below the floor.
Fill this against YOUR corpus. The calibration is only as good as this set, so:
- Use enough of each label (aim ~15+ per label).
- The signal lives in the BORDERLINE queries (e.g. 'make a cup of coffee'): label them by
your judgement of whether the corpus is meant to cover them, then see which side of the
- Run AFTER the corpus is near its final size (a floor tuned on a partial corpus won't hold).
Entries whose query starts with '<' are placeholders and are skipped by the script.

queries.label: off_corpus
queries.note: clearly out of domain — anchor for the off_corpus cluster

queries.label: grounded
queries.note: BORDERLINE — set label to what the corpus is MEANT to cover

queries.query: <add a grounded query the corpus clearly covers>
queries.label: grounded

queries.query: <add a borderline daily-living task and label it grounded/off_corpus by intent>
queries.label: grounded
